events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 0,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Electrical cords across walkways creating trip hazards and potential for cord damage/electrocution",
    "Missing guardrails on elevated surfaces exposing workers to fall hazards",
    "Blocked egress paths and fire extinguishers impeding emergency response",
    "Improvised scaffolds not built to OSHA 1926 Subpart L standards risking collapse and falls",
    "Unattended open holes presenting fall-through hazards"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately reroute all electrical cords overhead or use approved cord protectors/ramps; de-energize if damaged",
    "Install temporary guardrails meeting 1926.451/1926.502 requirements on all open sides and edges",
    "Clear all egress paths and ensure immediate access to fire extinguishers; post signage",
    "Dismantle improvised scaffolds; erect only compliant scaffolds per 1926 Subpart L with competent person inspection",
    "Cover or barricade all open holes immediately with secured covers marked 'HOLE' or standard guardrails"
  ],
  "rationale": "Multiple imminent danger conditions exist simultaneously: trip/electrocution from cords, falls from missing guardrails and open holes, scaffold collapse, and blocked emergency access. These violate OSHA general duty clause and specific 1926 Subpart L scaffold standards. Work must stop until hazards are abated.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA General Duty Clause Section 5(a)(1)",
    "1926 Subpart L - Scaffolds",
    "1926.451 - General requirements for scaffolds",
    "1926.502 - Fall protection systems criteria",
    "1926.501 - Duty to have fall protection",
    "1926.416 - Electrical safety-related work practices",
    "1926.34 - Means of egress",
    "1926.150 - Fire protection"
  ]
}
